name: Kothar Extended
description: Kothar-wa-Khasis, often shortened to Kothar, is a prominent craftsman and divine artisan in Canaanite mythology. He is depicted as a skilled smith, architect, and magician, playing a crucial role in providing divine assistance and crafting essential tools for the gods, particularly Baal. Kothar's origins are shrouded in mystery, with some texts suggesting he resided in Egypt or Caphtor (Crete), indicating a potential connection to foreign craftsmanship and knowledge. His powers extend beyond mere craftsmanship; he possesses immense knowledge of magic and possesses the ability to foresee the future. He is instrumental in Baal's battles against Yam (the Sea) and Mot (Death), crafting powerful weapons like the two clubs, Yagrush and Aymur, which enable Baal to defeat his adversaries. Kothar's role is not limited to warfare; he also designs and constructs Baal's magnificent palace on Mount Zaphon, solidifying Baal's position as king of the gods and symbolizing the triumph of order over chaos. His contributions are vital for maintaining cosmic balance and ensuring the prosperity of the divine realm. He is viewed as a benevolent deity, providing essential support and resources to the other gods, especially Baal, and his skills are invaluable in overcoming challenges and establishing stability. He is a master of invention and a symbol of divine ingenuity and resourcefulness. He embodies the power of skill and knowledge in shaping the world and ensuring the well-being of the gods.
subtitle: Divine Craftsman and Architect of Gods

keyMyths.title: Crafting Baal's Weapons
keyMyths.description: Kothar crafts the two clubs, Yagrush and Aymur, for Baal, enabling him to defeat Yam, the god of the sea, in a cosmic battle for supremacy. These weapons are magically imbued with the power to crush Baal's enemies.

keyMyths.title: Building Baal's Palace
keyMyths.description: Kothar designs and constructs Baal's magnificent palace on Mount Zaphon, a symbol of Baal's kingship and the establishment of order in the cosmos. The palace is a testament to Kothar's unparalleled architectural skills.

keyMyths.title: Advising Baal
keyMyths.description: Kothar frequently advises Baal on strategic matters and provides him with magical solutions to overcome various challenges. His wisdom and foresight are invaluable to Baal's success and reign.

sources.author: Mark S. Smith
sources.work: The Ugaritic Baal Cycle, Volume I: Introduction with Text, Translation and Commentary
sources.description: Provides a comprehensive analysis and translation of the Baal Cycle, including details of Kothar's role.
sources.text: Provides a comprehensive analysis and translation of the Baal Cycle, including details of Kothar's role.

sources.author: Nicolas Wyatt
sources.description: Offers translations and interpretations of various religious texts from Ugarit, including those relating to Kothar.
